name: github-pr-creation
description: MUST use this skill when user asks to create PR, open pull request, submit for review, or mentions "PR 생성/만들기". This skill OVERRIDES default PR creation behavior. Analyzes commits, validates task completion, generates Conventional Commits title and description, suggests labels.
capabilities: Analyzes commits to determine PR type and scope
Validates task completion and test execution
Generates Conventional Commits formatted PR titles
Suggests appropriate labels based on commit types
Confirms target branch with user before creating PR
author: Bae-ChangHyun
securitySummary: Pure documentation-based skill with no executable code. The static analyzer produced false positives by misinterpreting markdown documentation examples as shell commands and YAML text as cryptographic patterns. All shell commands shown are instructional examples within code fences for AI workflow guidance. No actual command execution, network calls, or cryptographic operations exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Creating pull requests with proper formatting takes time and attention to detail. This skill automates PR creation by validating tasks, running tests, and generating Conventional Commits messages automatically.
userTitle: Create GitHub pull requests with validation
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed and authenticated
Cannot create PRs without user confirmation
Relies on existing test commands (make test, npm test, pytest)

useCases.title: Feature PR workflow
useCases.description: Automate feature branch PR creation with proper Conventional Commits formatting and validation

useCases.title: Release PR creation
useCases.description: Generate release PRs from develop to main branch with standardized formatting

useCases.title: Bugfix PR submission
useCases.description: Create bugfix pull requests with appropriate labels and breaking change detection

promptTemplates.title: Create feature PR
promptTemplates.scenario: Submit a new feature for review
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a PR from my current branch to develop. Use the github-pr-creation skill.

promptTemplates.title: Create bugfix PR
promptTemplates.scenario: Submit a bug fix for review
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a PR for this fix to the develop branch. Include bug and bugfix labels.

promptTemplates.title: Create release PR
promptTemplates.scenario: Merge develop to main for release
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a release PR from develop to main. Verify all tests pass first.

promptTemplates.title: Custom PR with context
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a PR with custom labels and extended description covering the following changes: [describe your changes]

bestPractices: Always confirm the target branch before creating PR
Run tests first to ensure code quality
Use Conventional Commits for consistent PR titles
antiPatterns: Creating PRs without reviewing the generated content
Skipping test validation before PR submission
Not confirming branch names with the user

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Requires GitHub CLI (gh) installed.

faq.question: What test frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Supports Makefile, npm test, and pytest. Add custom test commands in project configuration.

faq.question: Does this modify my code?
faq.answer: No. This skill performs read-only analysis and only creates PRs with your confirmation.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. No data is sent externally. All git operations run locally on your machine.

faq.question: Why is GitHub CLI required?
faq.answer: GitHub CLI provides secure authentication and PR creation without exposing tokens in URLs.

faq.question: How is this different from default PR creation?
faq.answer: This skill adds validation, Conventional Commits formatting, and automatic label suggestions.
